risks: No private endpoints or VNet isolation — public network access is enabled on ML workspace, Log Analytics, and storage accounts, exposing the pipeline to internet-facing attack surface
Storage accounts use Standard_LRS (locally redundant only); a single datacenter failure could cause data loss for training data and ML artifacts
Encryption uses Microsoft-managed keys by default; user goal explicitly requires encrypting all outputs, but CMK via Key Vault is only documented as an upgrade path, not configured
Storage account local auth (shared key access) is not explicitly disabled, which conflicts with Azure best practices that mandate disabling key-based access in favor of RBAC/managed identity
No resource tagging strategy defined for cost tracking, environment classification, or operational management
Basic ACR SKU has 10 GiB storage limit and does not support private link or content trust, limiting production hardening options
correctionsRecommended: Disable local auth (allowSharedKeyAccess: false) on both storage accounts to enforce managed-identity-only access per Azure best practices
Add private endpoints for Key Vault, both storage accounts, ML workspace, and Container Registry to enable network-isolated access
Add a VNet and subnet resources to serve as the network isolation foundation for private endpoints
Consider upgrading storage redundancy from Standard_LRS to Standard_ZRS for zone-level resilience on production training data
Configure customer-managed key (CMK) encryption on storage accounts using the existing Key Vault to fully satisfy the encrypt-all-outputs requirement
Upgrade Container Registry from Basic to at least Standard (or Premium for private link) to support production workload storage and network isolation needs
Add a resource tagging convention (e.g., environment, project, cost-center) across all resources for operational governance
